verdict_reasoning: VEGFA encodes a secreted growth factor (signal peptide→Golgi→constitutive secretion) with no TM domain, GPI anchor, or outer-leaflet lipidation in any isoform. Shorter isoforms (VEGF121) are freely diffusible; longer heparin-binding isoforms (VEGF165, VEGF189) associate non-covalently with HSPGs on the cell surface and ECM, but this interaction is reversible and stays in equilibrium with the soluble pool — it washes off and does not constitute wash-resistant TM-partner anchoring. Clinical anti-VEGFA programs (bevacizumab, ranibizumab, aflibercept, pegaptanib) all target the soluble circulating pool, not a cell-surface-displayed protein body. Pre-no checklist: (1) no cell-surface-directed therapeutic targeting the protein body on the PM; (2) no TM precursor/ectodomain-shedding architecture; (3) HSPG binding is reversible — fails wash test; (4) no documented PM minority pool via cycling, lysosomal exocytosis, or ER-PM junctions; (5) no documented cancer/stress/activation/cell-death outer-leaflet protein-body display; no narrow germline/developmental surface display via own anchor.
